section.heading: Surveys of Coast Guard families

subsection.heading: Authority.—
subsection.chapeau: The Commandant, in order to determine the effectiveness of Federal policies, programs, and activities related to the families of Coast Guard members, may survey—

paragraph.content: the immediate family of any Coast Guard member or retired Coast Guard member; and
any survivor of a deceased Coast Guard member.

subsection.heading: Voluntary Participation.—
subsection.content: Participation in any survey conducted under subsection (a) shall be voluntary.

subsection.heading: Federal Recordkeeping.—
subsection.content: Each individual surveyed under subsection (a) shall be considered an employee of the United States for purposes of section 3502(3)(A)(i) of title 44.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 113–281, title II, § 214(a), Dec. 18, 2014, 128 Stat. 3029, § 532; renumbered § 2902, Pub. L. 115–282, title I, § 117(b), Dec. 4, 2018, 132 Stat. 4230; amended Pub. L. 116–283, div. G, title LVXXXV [LXXXV], § 8505(a)(47), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4750.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 2902 was renumbered section 5102 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–282 renumbered section 532 of this title as this section.
